description: Reusable Azure workload building blocks for synthesizing managed-disk resource-list inventories for the Everpure Azure TCO tool. Two families: (1) SQL Server tiers (sql_{small,medium,large}_{v1,v2}) — a 200 GiB OS disk + data/log/tempdb; the v1 variant uses Premium SSD v1 (Premium_LRS, tier-derived performance) for the data path, the v2 variant uses provisioned Premium SSD v2 (PremiumV2_LRS) — small/medium/large scale ~0.5×/1×/2×, and each v1/v2 pair shares the same capacities; (2) general-purpose common servers (common_server_*) — a 200 GiB OS disk + a single tier-derived Premium SSD v1 data disk (512 GiB / 1.5 TiB / 3 TiB). Drop a model's 'drive_config' into a generate_inventory.py spec archetype (the 'disks' array), set region/subscriptions/zones/vnets + instance count, and expand into a fleet CSV.

baseline_reference.source: Innocap SQL-on-Azure analysis, storage build-out (per instance)
baseline_reference.note: Original build-out. The models below revise the LOG drive to equal the DATA drive in capacity at half its performance, so the medium tier's data-path totals differ from this origin figure.

conventions.generator: tools/generate_inventory.py expands these archetypes into the resource-list CSV the tool ingests.

disk_sku_values.PremiumV2_LRS: Premium SSD v2 — provisioned IOPS/MBps; carries the model's performance (set iops + mbps).
disk_sku_values.Premium_LRS: Premium SSD v1 — tier-derived performance; do NOT set iops/mbps.

conventions.disk_type_gotcha: The tool matches disk type by case-insensitive SUBSTRING in this order: 'ultra','standardssd','standard','premiumv2','premium'. Always use the *_LRS SKU names above. 'Premium SSD v2' (with a space) does NOT contain 'premiumv2' and would be MISREAD as Premium v1 — never use friendly names.
conventions.provisioned_only: Only PremiumV2_LRS and UltraSSD_LRS accept 'iops'/'mbps'. The generator rejects iops/mbps on any other type.
conventions.os_disk: Each model has one 200 GiB Premium_LRS OS/root disk (root:true). It is realistic filler and is excluded from the IOPS/MBps data-path totals.
conventions.log_sizing: The log drive matches the data drive's capacity and runs at half its IOPS and half its MBps.
conventions.performance_totals: The headline 'totals' below are the SQL DATA PATH (data + log + tempdb provisioned disks); the 200 GiB OS disk is excluded.
conventions.role_key: Each provisioned drive carries a 'role' (data|log|tempdb) for readability; generate_inventory.py ignores unknown keys, so it passes through harmlessly.

sql_small_v1.label: SQL Small (v1) — Premium SSD v1 data/log, ~0.5× baseline

sql_small_v2.label: SQL Small (v2) — Premium SSD v2 data/log, ~0.5× baseline

suggested_compute.note: E8bds_v5 uncached remote-disk limit ~24,200 IOPS / 680 MBps — covers 12k / 425.

sql_medium_v1.label: SQL Medium (v1) — Premium SSD v1 data/log, baseline tier

sql_medium_v2.label: SQL Medium (v2) — Premium SSD v2 data/log, baseline tier

suggested_compute.note: E16bds_v5 uncached remote-disk limit is well above 24k / 850; matches the §2 benchmark VM.

sql_large_v1.label: SQL Large (v1) — Premium SSD v1 data/log, ~2× baseline

sql_large_v2.label: SQL Large (v2) — Premium SSD v2 data/log, ~2× baseline

suggested_compute.note: Verify E32bds_v5 uncached limits cover 48k IOPS / 1,700 MBps; step up to Standard_E48bds_v5 for headroom.

common_server_small.label: Common Server Small — general-purpose, 512 GiB Premium SSD v1 data

totals.performance: tier-derived — 512 GiB maps to Azure P20 (~2,300 IOPS / 150 MBps)

common_server_medium.label: Common Server Medium — general-purpose, 1.5 TiB Premium SSD v1 data

totals.performance: tier-derived — 1,536 GiB maps to Azure P40 (~7,500 IOPS / 250 MBps)

common_server_large.label: Common Server Large — general-purpose, 3 TiB Premium SSD v1 data

totals.performance: tier-derived — 3,072 GiB maps to Azure P50 (~7,500 IOPS / 250 MBps)

usage.step_1: Copy one or more model 'drive_config' blocks into a generate_inventory.py spec — each becomes an archetype under 'disks[]' with its own region/subscriptions/zones/vnets and num_instances_in_each_subscription_zone_combination.
usage.step_3: Upload the CSV in the tool (Disk Type + Disk Size auto-map), then Run Analysis to get the Azure managed disk vs Everpure Cloud TCO.
usage.example_spec: tools/innocap_3tier_spec.json (a ready fleet: 8x small + 4x medium + 2x large)
